question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/Downloader.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: partialCollection()] Can an unprivileged external user, by controlling or poisoning the stake values reported for consensus nodes, cause partialCollection() to select a biased subset of nodes that excludes nodes holding specific transactions, thereby preventing those transactions from ever being gossiped to the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/Downloader.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: partialCollection()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that Collections.shuffle() is seeded by a predictable or manipulable source to deterministically influence which nodes are included in the partial collection, causing targeted transactions from excluded nodes to be silently dropped?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/Downloader.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: partialCollection()] Can an unprivileged external user, given that partialCollection() uses getFirst().getTotalStake() to compute neededStake, trigger a NullPointerException or incorrect stake calculation by injecting a node with zero or null totalStake, causing the loop to never terminate or select zero nodes, preventing all transaction gossip?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/Downloader.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: partialCollection()] Can an unprivileged external user, by causing all consensus nodes to report zero individual stake values, force partialCollection() into an infinite loop (aggregateStake never reaches neededStake), causing a denial-of-service that prevents any transactions from being gossiped?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/Downloader.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: downloadAndParseSigFiles()] Can an unprivileged external user, by flooding the stream file provider with malformed or oversized signature files, exploit the onErrorContinue() handler to silently suppress all signature download errors, causing the sigFilesMap to be empty and all transactions in that batch to be permanently skipped?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/Downloader.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: downloadAndParseSigFiles()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger the timeout() operator on the Flux pipeline by delaying responses from enough nodes to exceed downloaderProperties.getCommon().getTimeout(), causing the entire batch of signature files to be dropped and the corresponding transactions to never be gossiped?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/Downloader.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: downloadAndParseSigFiles()] Can an unprivileged external user, by injecting duplicate or conflicting StreamFileSignature entries into the multimap via a compromised node, cause the TreeMultimap with STREAM_FILE_SIGNATURE_COMPARATOR to silently deduplicate legitimate signatures, reducing the count below the consensus threshold and preventing transaction gossip?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/Downloader.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: downloadAndParseSigFiles()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that listLimit is set to Long.MAX_VALUE when enabled, by injecting an enormous number of fake signature filenames into the provider listing, causing memory exhaustion in the Multimap and preventing legitimate transaction signatures from being processed?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/Downloader.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: verifySigsAndDownloadDataFiles()] Can an unprivileged external user, by ensuring that every signature group in sigFilesMap throws a SignatureVerificationException, exploit the continue logic to skip all groups except the last one, which then re-throws the exception, causing all transactions in the batch to be permanently suppressed?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/Downloader.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: verifySigsAndDownloadDataFiles()] Can an unprivileged external user, by injecting a crafted signature file that causes nodeSignatureVerifier.verify() to throw a non-SignatureVerificationException (e.g., RuntimeException), bypass the specific catch block and propagate the error to downloadNextBatch()'s generic catch, causing the entire batch to be silently logged and dropped?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/Downloader.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: verifySigsAndDownloadDataFiles()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that ShutdownHelper.isStopping() is checked at the top of the loop to race a shutdown signal with batch processing, causing the loop to exit mid-batch and permanently skip transactions that were in the remaining signature groups?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/Downloader.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: verifySigsAndDownloadDataFiles()] Can an unprivileged external user, by crafting signature files that all fail nodeSignatureVerifier.verify() for every group except the last, force the last group to throw a SignatureVerificationException that propagates up to downloadNextBatch() and is only logged as a warning, causing all transactions in the batch to be silently dropped?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/Downloader.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: verifySignatures()] Can an unprivileged external user, by ensuring that no signature in the collection has status CONSENSUS_REACHED, cause verifySignatures() to iterate through all signatures without processing any, return false, and log an error without retrying, permanently preventing the corresponding transactions from being gossiped?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/Downloader.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: verifySignatures()] Can an unprivileged external user, by injecting a crafted data file that causes streamFileReader.read() to throw an unchecked exception not caught by the inner catch blocks, cause verifySignatures() to propagate the exception upward and skip all remaining signatures in the collection, preventing transaction gossip?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/Downloader.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: verifySignatures()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the endDate check (dataFilename.getInstant().isAfter(endDate)) by crafting a signature file with a filename timestamp far in the future, causing downloaderProperties.setEnabled(false) to be triggered and permanently halting all future transaction gossip?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/Downloader.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: verifySignatures()] Can an unprivileged external user, by causing streamFileProvider.get(dataFilename).block() to return null for all CONSENSUS_REACHED signatures, trigger a NullPointerException via Objects.requireNonNull(), which is caught by the generic Exception handler, causing all signatures to fail and the transactions to be permanently skipped?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/Downloader.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: verifySignatures()] Can an unprivileged external user, by injecting a TransientProviderException for every CONSENSUS_REACHED node's data file, cause verifySignatures() to log a warning for each and return false without any retry mechanism, permanently dropping the transactions associated with that stream file?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/Downloader.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: verify()] Can an unprivileged external user, by crafting a stream file where getPreviousHash() returns a value that does not match the expectedPrevHash stored in lastStreamFile, cause verify() to throw a HashMismatchException that propagates to verifySignatures()'s generic catch, causing the node to be skipped and potentially all nodes to fail, preventing transaction gossip?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/Downloader.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: verify()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that verify() calls verifyHash() for both fileHash and metadataHash sequentially, by crafting a file where the fileHash matches but the metadataHash does not, causing a HashMismatchException that silently skips the node and potentially prevents the transaction from being gossiped if all nodes fail?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/Downloader.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: verifyHashChain()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the SHA_384.isHashEmpty(expectedPreviousHash) bypass in verifyHashChain() by causing the lastStreamFile to be cleared or reset to empty, making expectedPreviousHash null/empty and bypassing hash chain verification, allowing a forged stream file with arbitrary transactions to be accepted?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/Downloader.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: verifyHashChain()] Can an unprivileged external user, by injecting a stream file of a type where isChained() returns false, bypass verifyHashChain() entirely (it returns true unconditionally), allowing a forged file with suppressed or fabricated transactions to pass hash chain verification?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/Downloader.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: verifyHashChain()] Can an unprivileged external user, by manipulating the stream file's previousHash field to match the expectedPreviousHash stored in lastStreamFile while altering the file's actual transaction content, pass verifyHashChain() and inject a file with suppressed transactions that appears valid to the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/Downloader.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getStartAfterFilename()] Can an unprivileged external user, by causing dateRangeCalculator.getLastStreamFile() to return a StreamFile with a very recent or future timestamp as its name, cause getStartAfterFilename() to return a filename far ahead in the sequence, causing the downloader to skip all intermediate stream files and permanently lose the transactions they contain?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/Downloader.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getStartAfterFilename()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the compareAndSet(Optional.empty(), streamFile) race condition in getStartAfterFilename() by triggering concurrent download threads, causing lastStreamFile to be set to a stale or incorrect value that skips a range of stream files containing specific transactions?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/Downloader.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getStartAfterFilename()] Can an unprivileged external user, by causing StreamFilename.from() to throw an exception when parsing the last stream file's name, cause getStartAfterFilename() to propagate the exception and abort the entire downloadNextBatch() cycle, preventing all transactions in the next batch from being gossiped?
